title: Early Intervention for Children With Disabilities/Delays - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Early Intervention for Children With Disabilities/Delays 29 Matching Service Providers 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details BLACK PARENT INITIATIVE SAWUBONA Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides culturally specific services and information to promote optimal health. On site food and child care are available for scheduled events. Direct services include: -Parenting support and educational services aimed at mobilizing Black parents to ensure their children achieve educational excellence -Employment services: development, placement, and support -Family Literacy and STEAM fairs -Cultivating Culture: Gardening and healthy eating program -Advocacy for African American families with young children in need of early intervention/early childhood special education -Parent University classes and experiences focused on: Parental engagement in children's education, effective parenting, and all areas of optimal health (intellectual, physical, emotional, spiritual, socio-economic, and relationships) 901 NE Glisan Street Suite 250 Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm. Classes are usually held Thursday nights. Email (503) 284-8000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BRIDGEWAY HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers developmental therapies and supports including in-home occupational and speech therapy, social groups, classes, and tutoring. Also offers parent support groups, parent and child activity groups, parenting classes, and specialized information and referral. 555 E 15th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Families with children living with autism and related disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 345-0805 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT EARLY INTERVENTION/EARLY CHILDHOOD SPECIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Oregon Department of Education contracts with local agencies to provide a statewide system of free services for young children with developmental delays and disabilities and their families, including: -Early Intervention (EI): Individually designed services for children birth to three and support for parents to enhance children's physical, cognitive, communication, social emotional, and/or adaptive development; and -Early Childhood Special Education (ECSE): Specially designed instruction for children ages 3 to the age of public school eligibility in the areas of communication, cognitive, social/emotional, adaptive, and others. 13455 SE 97th Avenue Clackamas, OR 97015 Eligibility: Any child 0-school age with a suspected developmental delay or disability is eligible for free screening and evaluation. Children determined to have delays or disabilities are eligible for EI/ECSE services. Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4:30pm Email (503) 675-4041 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DAVID DOUGLAS SCHOOL DISTRICT 40 EARLY INTERVENTION AND EARLY CHILDHOOD SPECIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Individually designed services for children and support for parents to enhance children's physical, cognitive, communication, social emotional, and/or adaptive development. 11300 NE Halsey Street Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Ages 0-5 with a suspected developmental delay or disability Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 386-4919 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOUGLAS EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT EARLY INTERVENTION AND EARLY CHILDHOOD SPECIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Early Intervention (EI): Individually designed services for children and support for parents to enhance children's physical, cognitive, communication, social emotional and/or adaptive development. Early Childhood Special Education (ECSE): Specially designed instruction for children. Support offered in the areas of communication, cognitive, social/emotional, adaptive and others. 1409 NE Diamond Lake Boulevard Suite 110 Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Any child 0-school age with a suspected developmental delay or disability is eligible for free screening and evaluation. Children determined to have delays or disabilities are eligible for EI and ECSE services Hours: Monday-Friday 8am to 4pm Email (541) 440-4794 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOUGLAS EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT JACKSON COUNTY EI/ECSE PROGRAM EARLY INTERVENTION AND EARLY CHILDHOOD SPECIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Early Intervention (EI): Individually designed services for children and support for parents to enhance children's physical, cognitive, communication, social emotional and/or adaptive development. Early Childhood Special Education (ECSE): Specially designed instruction for children. Support offered in the areas of communication, cognitive, social/emotional, adaptive and others. 640 Superior Court Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Any child 0-school age with a suspected developmental delay or disability is eligible for free screening and evaluation. Children determined to have delays or disabilities are eligible for EI and ECSE services Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 494-7859 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EARLY CHILDHOOD CARES EARLY INTERVENTION/EARLY CHILDHOOD SPECIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Supports infants, toddlers, preschoolers, and their families by providing early intervention and early childhood special education to all eligible children with developmental delays and/or disabilities in Lane County. 1500 W 12th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Eligibility is based on state criteria for Early Intervention and Early Childhood Special Education services Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 346-2578 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY LANE COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides families with a comprehensive set of early childhood and parenting programs, including case management, home visitation, respite care, early intervention, and early childhood special education. Also provides early childhood therapeutic classrooms. Programs for participating parents include a clothing closet, parent education groups, support groups, parent training, a parent resource library, and referrals to child passenger seat programs. 720 N 14th Street Cottage Grove, OR 97424 Eligibility: Families with children ages 6 weeks-6 years -Therapeutic classrooms: Ages 6 weeks-4 years -Respite care and other services: Ages 6 weeks-6 years Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 942-4835 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY NORTH DOUGLAS COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides families with a comprehensive set of early childhood and parenting programs, including case management, home visitation, respite care, early intervention, and early childhood special education. Also provides early childhood therapeutic classrooms. Programs for participating parents include a clothing closet, parent education groups, support groups, parent training, a parent resource library, and referrals to child passenger seat programs. 239 East B Avenue Drain, OR 97435 Eligibility: Families with children ages 6 weeks-6 years -Therapeutic classrooms: Ages 6 weeks-4 years -Respite care and other services: Ages 6 weeks-6 years Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 942-4835 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY SOUTH LANE COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides families with a comprehensive set of early childhood and parenting programs, including case management, home visitation, respite care, early intervention, and early childhood special education. Also provides early childhood therapeutic classrooms. Programs for participating parents include a clothing closet, parent education groups, support groups, parent training, a parent resource library, and referrals to child passenger seat programs. 75 S 4th street Creswell, OR 97426 Eligibility: Families with children ages 6 weeks-6 years -Therapeutic classrooms: Ages 6 weeks-4 years -Respite care and other services: Ages 6 weeks-6 years Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 942-4835 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HARNEY COUNTY EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT EARLY CHILDHOOD CENTER CHILDHOOD SPECIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers services and support to families with children diagnosed with developmental disabilities or experiencing developmental delays. Services include home visits, parent-toddler groups, structured classes, direct therapy, help developing an Individual Family Service Plan (IFSP), and information and connection to community resources. 655 W Fillmore Street Burns, OR 97720 Eligibility: Children ages birth to kindergarten-age (0-5) who demonstrate a significant developmental delay in one or more areas and/or meet disability eligibility criteria in one of the following categories: -Communication Disorder -Autism Spectrum Disorder -Hearing Impairment -Visual Impairment -Orthopedic Impairment -Other Health Impairments -Specific Learning Disability -Emotional Disturbance -Intellectual Disability -Deaf Blind -Traumatic Brain Injury Hours: No standard hours (541) 573-6461 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HIGH DESERT EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT EARLY INTERVENTION/EARLY CHILDHOOD SPECIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Contracts with local agencies to provide a statewide system of free services for young children with developmental delays and disabilities and their families, including; Early Intervention (EI): Individually designed services for children and support for parents to enhance children's physical, cognitive, communication, social emotional and/or adaptive development; and Early Childhood Special Education (ECSE): Specially designed instruction for children in the areas of communication, cognitive, social/emotional, adaptive, and others. 2804 SW 6th Street Redmond, OR 97756 Eligibility: Any child 0-school age with a suspected developmental delay or disability is eligible for free screening and evaluation, children determined to have delays or disabilities are eligible for EI/ECSE services Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 312-1963 , Get Directions Visit Website More Details INTERMOUNTAIN EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT EARLY INTERVENTION/EARLY CHILDHOOD SPECIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Oregon Department of Education contracts with local agencies to provide a statewide system of free services for young children with developmental delays and disabilities and their families, including: -Early Intervention (EI): Individually designed services for children birth to three and support for parents to enhance children's physical, cognitive, communication, social emotional, and/or adaptive development; and -Early Childhood Special Education (ECSE): Specially designed instruction for children ages 3 to the age of public school eligibility in the areas of communication, cognitive, social/emotional, adaptive, and others. 2001 SW Nye Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Any child 0-school age with a suspected developmental delay or disability is eligible for free screening and evaluation. Children determined to have delays or disabilities are eligible for EI/ECSE services. Support for parents to enhance children's physical, cognitive, social, emotional and/or adaptive development. 5825 NE Ray Circle Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Any child 0-school age with a suspected developmental delay or disability is eligible for free screening and evaluation, children determined to have delays or disabilities are eligible for the Early Intervention (EI) or Early Childhood Special Education program (ECSE) Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (503) 614-1446 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST REGIONAL EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT NORTHWEST REGIONAL EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT CLATSOP SERVICE CENTER EARLY INTERVENTION AND EARLY CHILDHOOD SPECIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Services for young children with developmental delays and disabilities. Support for parents to enhance children's physical, cognitive, social, emotional and/or adaptive development. 800 Port Avenue Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: Any child 0-school age with a suspected developmental delay or disability is eligible for free screening and evaluation, children determined to have delays or disabilities are eligible for the Early Intervention (EI) or Early Childhood Special Education program (ECSE) Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (503) 366-1251 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST REGIONAL EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT NORTHWEST REGIONAL EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT TILLAMOOK SERVICE CENTER EARLY INTERVENTION AND EARLY CHILDHOOD SPECIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Services for young children with developmental delays and disabilities. Support for parents to enhance children's physical, cognitive, social, emotional and/or adaptive development. 2515 3rd Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Any child 0-school age with a suspected developmental delay or disability is eligible for free screening and evaluation, children determined to have delays or disabilities are eligible for the Early Intervention (EI) or Early Childhood Special Education program (ECSE) Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (503) 842-8243 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates a call-in center for family members of children and youth with special needs and chronic health conditions (developmental and emotional disabilities, behavioral problems, and chronic physical illnesses) and professionals who serve them. The center specializes in providing information and resources related to health information, health care access, and special needs systems and supports. All calls are confidential. Families may also request to be placed on an email list to receive monthly correspondence about issues of importance to children and youth with special needs in Oregon. 707 SW Gaines Street Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Family members of individuals age 26 and younger with special physical, emotional, behavioral, or mental health needs, including those who are at risk or suspected of having a special need. The child/youth does not have to have a diagnosed condition. Does not require documentation to prove citizenship to receive services. Hours: Message line available 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (833) 990-9930 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON SCREENING PROJECT DEVELOPMENTAL SCREENING QUESTIONNAIRES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Confidential website where parents and caregivers can check a child’s development by completing an online questionnaire. No personal identifying information is required. Results letters, completed questionnaires, learning activities, follow-up resources, parenting materials, and community referrals are available by email or download. The site also provides contact information for Oregon Early Intervention providers and a Developmental Screening Toolkit for professionals who support families. A project coordinator monitors the site daily and is available to parents, caregivers, and providers by phone or email. Website Eligibility: Parents and primary caregivers of children ages 1 month-6 years Hours: Not applicable Email (541) 346-2580 Visit Website More Details PROVIDENCE HEALTH AND SERVICES OREGON AND SOUTHWEST WASHINGTON SWINDELLS RESOURCE CENTER HELP ME GROW OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers supports for families, especially those who experience behavioral, developmental, and complex medical concerns. Connects parents and caregivers to parent education classes, sibling support groups, and inclusive developmental playgroups. 9135 SW Barnes Road Portland, OR 97225 Eligibility: Families with children 0-5 years and pregnant individuals in any trimester Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (883) 868-4769 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TUCKER MAXON SCHOOL EARLY INTERVENTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Focuses on developing collaborative partnerships with parents and children that facilitate spoken language development through listening. Offers intensive auditory-oral intervention, on-site audiologist who does hearing aid analysis and cochlear implant mapping, regular home and school sessions, facilitated toddler groups with both children with typical hearing and children who are deaf and support and information for families. 2860 SE Holgate Boulevard Portland, OR 97202 Eligibility: Newborns to age 3 with a hearing impairment Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (503) 235-6551 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WILLAMETTE EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT WILLAMETTE ESD MARION CENTER EARLY LEARNING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Oregon Department of Education contracts with local agencies to provide a statewide system of free services for young children with developmental delays and disabilities and their families, including: -Early Intervention (EI): Individually designed services for children birth to three and support for parents to enhance children's physical, cognitive, communication, social emotional and/or adaptive development; and -Early Childhood Special Education (ECSE): Specially designed instruction for children ages 3 to the age of public school eligibility in the areas of communication, cognitive, social/emotional, adaptive and others. 2611 Pringle Road SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: Children age 0-5 with a suspected developmental delay or disability is eligible for free screening and evaluation. Children determined to have delays or disabilities are eligible for EI/ECSE services.
